content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves gently to the left ahead with the car well-positioned near center, continuing along the left-curving section heading southwest.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 61, target = 61 + 5 = 66
- Waypoint 66: (3.2950, 4.3193)
- Car position: (3.9763, 3.6305)
- Bearing = atan2(4.3193 - 3.6305, 3.2950 - 3.9763) = atan2(0.6888, -0.6813)
- = 180 - atan2(0.6888, 0.6813) = 180 - 45.31° = 134.69°
- Current heading: 141.78°
- Delta = 134.69 - 141.78 = -7.09° → slight right correction needed

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves left ahead but waypoint 66 is slightly right of current heading (delta=-7.09°), so a small -5° steering correction maintains the waypoint line.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track has a left-curving section around waypoints 55-70, heading southwest",
    "Car is consistently slightly right of center (~0.07-0.12m) but within safe margin (track width 0.76m)",
    "Full circuit ~17.7m, speed 1.5 is max and appropriate for moderate curves",
    "Use steering_angle=0 for |delta| <5° to avoid oscillation on near-straight sections",
    "Delta near -5 to -9° means gentle right correction — use steering_angle=-5",
    "Delta near +5 to +9° means gentle left correction — use steering_angle=+5",
    "Car is maintaining 1.5 speed well through this section",
    "Waypoints 60-70 show continuing left curve (south-southwest to west)",
    "5° steering is appropriate for delta 5-10° range to avoid overshoot",
    "When delta drops below 5°, immediately switch to 0 steering to prevent oscillation"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [4.207, 4.133, 4.055, 3.976],
    "y": [3.4532, 3.5094, 3.5685, 3.6305],
    "yaw": [141.464, 141.698, 141.858, 141.781]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": -7.09,
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": -5.0
}
```
